detail: behavioural shift,behavioral shift,altered behaviour,altered behavior,flight behaviour,flight behavior,avoidance,novel behaviour,behavioural consequence,scaring away
rationale: Fable evidence repeatedly uses 'behavioral shift', 'altered behavior', 'flight behavior' and plain 'avoidance' which the current change/response-only terms miss.

detail: revenue,price per kg,market trend,economic profit,net profit,income loss,monetary contribution,economic cost,high prices,commodity value
rationale: Papers express economic impact via revenue, prices, profits and income loss rather than the narrow 'economic value'/'WTP' set currently listed.

detail: trophic cascade,cascade effect,ontogenetic dietary shift,ontogenetic shift in diet,dietary flexibility,trophic plasticity,predation pressure,predator-prey,forage for dependent species
rationale: Fable flags trophic-cascade and ontogenetic-diet-shift language that the four current phrases do not capture.

detail: parturition,parturition season,pupping site,spawning site,birthing area,egg-laying habitat,egg case nursery
rationale: Most missed papers describe 'parturition' seasons/sites and pupping/spawning sites not covered by the four ground/area-only terms.

detail: BRUV,baited remote underwater video,aerial survey,aerial video surveillance,bottom trawl survey,fishery-independent sampling,fisheries-independent survey,sampling cruise,underwater videography,visual survey,vessel transect
rationale: Survey papers use BRUV (singular), aerial/trawl surveys and fishery-independent sampling phrasing the current list omits.

detail: post-release survival,post-release survivorship,post-release fishing mortality,discard survival,post-capture mortality,mortality after capture
rationale: Missed papers use post-release survival/survivorship and discard/post-capture mortality synonyms absent from the current four terms.

detail: length-frequency,size class,catch size distribution,biomass size spectrum,size spectrum,smaller and younger,length-converted catch curve
rationale: Size-structure evidence uses hyphenated 'length-frequency', 'size class' and 'size spectrum' phrasing the current spaced terms and anchors block.

detail: circular hook,circle hooks,Mustad circle,non-offset circle hook,circle vs J
rationale: Several papers use 'circular hooks' or comparative 'circle vs J-shaped hook' framing not matched by the two current terms.
Injury is described concretely as abrasions, lesions, lacerations, amputations and bite/rake marks that the current term set misses.

detail: dropline,angling,baited hook,hand line,rod and line,trap and line,fly rod,shark hook,hooked
rationale: Hook-and-line gear appears as droplines, angling, baited/shark hooks and rod-and-line variants beyond the six current terms.
